message:
Add SPM config and enhance TUI styles

New features:
- SPM (Subscription Provider Manager) configuration support
- config subcommand for managing provider settings
- Enhanced TUI styles with more color options

Changes:
- Simplified add.go logic
- Comprehensive SPM config tests
- README documentation for new features

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-cfb","title":"Usage analytics CLI command","description":"## Purpose
Add `caam usage` command to display profile usage statistics from the command line.

## Background
Users should be able to see their usage patterns to understand which profiles
they use most, identify underutilized accounts, and optimize their workflow.

## Command Structure
```bash
# Summary view (default)
caam usage

# Detailed view for specific profile
caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed

# Time range filtering
caam usage --days 30
caam usage --since 2025-12-01

# Output formats
caam usage --json
caam usage --csv
```

## Example Output
```
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs
gemini/default       3 sessions    0.5 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed
Session History for claude/work
───────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
...

Total: 42 sessions, 18.5 hours over 7 days
Average session: 26 minutes
Most active day: Monday (12 sessions)
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// cmd/caam/usage.go
var usageCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "usage",
    Short: "Display profile usage statistics",
    RunE:  runUsage,
}

func init() {
    usageCmd.Flags().StringP("profile", "p", "", "Profile to show (provider/name)")
    usageCmd.Flags().Bool("detailed", false, "Show detailed session history")
    usageCmd.Flags().Int("days", 7, "Number of days to include")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("format", "table", "Output format: table, json, csv")
}

func runUsage(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    db, err := db.Open()
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer db.Close()
    
    // Query and format based on flags
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/usage.go
- cmd/caam/usage_test.go
- Modify cmd/caam/root.go to add subcommand

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary shows all profiles with session count and duration
- [ ] Detailed view shows individual sessions for a profile
- [ ] Time range filtering works correctly
- [ ] JSON and CSV export work
- [ ] Handles empty database gracefully
- [ ] Performance acceptable for large activity logs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:22:53.646488393Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:11:12.338404628Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:11:12.338404628Z","close_reason":"Added caam usage command with summary/detailed views and json/csv output.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-cfb","depends_on_id":"caam-r36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:28:57.057526979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting caam usage command: implement cmd/caam/cmd/usage.go with summary + detailed profile view, since/days filtering, and table/json/csv output using internal/db.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:06:53Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented  command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:10:45Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"(Follow-up) Implemented the caam usage command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:11:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-ckk","title":"E2E: CLI command workflow tests","description":"End-to-end integration tests for CLI command workflows:

## Test Scenarios
1. `caam list` - List all profiles across providers
2. `caam backup <provider> <email>` - Backup current auth state
3. `caam restore <provider> <email>` - Restore saved profile
4. `caam delete <provider> <email>` - Delete a saved profile
5. `caam status` - Show current auth status for all providers

## Requirements
- Use t.TempDir() for isolated file system tests
- Real file operations (no mocks)
- Test actual cobra command execution
- Detailed logging at each step
- Test error cases (missing files, invalid profiles)

## Logging Format
Each test should log:
- [START] Test name and description
- [SETUP] Temp directory and fixture creation
- [EXEC] Command being executed
- [VERIFY] Expected vs actual results
- [CLEANUP] Resources cleaned up
- [PASS/FAIL] Final result with duration","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T06:52:40.357727069Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T07:15:21.938011493Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T07:15:21.938011493Z","close_reason":"Completed 9 E2E tests for CLI command workflows: list, backup, restore, delete, status, clear, profile workflow, invalid tool error handling, multi-provider isolation.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-7a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T06:54:53.822477208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ckk","depends_on_id":"caam-aek","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T06:55:26.082995053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ctw","title":"[EPIC] Utility Package Tests","description":"# Utility Package Tests

## Priority: P2 (MEDIUM)
Several utility packages have gaps in test coverage.

## Scope: Multiple packages

### 1. internal/browser/urldetect.go (5+ functions - UNTESTED)
- URL detection and parsing logic
- Browser URL handling

### 2. internal/project/matcher.go (UNTESTED)
```go
isGlob(pattern string) bool
matchingGlobs(associations map[string]map[string]string, target string) []globMatch
wildcardCount(pattern string) int
```

### 3. internal/signals/ (40% coverage)
Files without tests:
- signals.go (general signal handling)
- log.go (signal logging)
- Windows files: process_windows.go, signals_windows.go, send_windows.go

### 4. internal/refresh/url_guard.go (UNTESTED)
- URL validation and sanitization

## Test Requirements
- Test all URL parsing edge cases
- Test glob matching patterns
- Platform-specific signal tests where possible
- Detailed logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- 100% coverage for urldetect.go
- 100% coverage for matcher.go
- 80%+ coverage for signals (platform limitations acknowledged)
- 100% coverage for url_guard.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:59:12.250037852Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T03:05:22.162266559Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T03:05:22.162266559Z","close_reason":"Completed: urldetect.go already had tests, added matcher.go tests (80.2% coverage), signals at 81.6%, refresh at 52.9%. Fixed duplicate test function names.","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"caam-czda","title":"E2E integration tests for daemon lifecycle","description":"# E2E Integration Tests for Daemon Lifecycle

## Overview
End-to-end tests for the background daemon service including
startup, operation, signals, and shutdown.

## Workflow Tests Required

### 1. Daemon Startup Workflow
```
Start daemon → Verify PID file → Check status → Verify listening
```
- Tests daemon initialization
- PID file creation
- Status reporting

### 2. Token Refresh Cycle Workflow
```
Start → Create expiring profile → Wait for refresh → Verify new token
```
- Tests automatic refresh scheduling
- Token expiry detection
- Refresh execution

### 3. Signal Handling Workflow
```
Start → Send SIGHUP (reload) → Verify reload → Send SIGTERM → Graceful stop
```
- Tests signal reception
- Configuration reload
- Graceful shutdown

### 4. Backup Scheduling Workflow
```
Configure backup → Start daemon → Wait for backup → Verify bundle
```
- Tests automatic backup
- Backup rotation
- State persistence

### 5. Error Recovery Workflow
```
Start → Cause refresh error → Verify retry → Eventually succeed
```
- Tests error handling
- Exponential backoff
- Recovery behavior

### 6. Multi-Instance Prevention
```
Start daemon A → Try start daemon B → Verify rejection → Stop A → Start B
```
- Tests PID file locking
- Stale PID detection

## Implementation Notes
- Use short intervals (seconds) for timing tests
- Fork actual daemon process for realism
- Use channels/signals for coordination
- Clean up processes in defer

## Files to Create
- internal/e2e/workflows/daemon_lifecycle_test.go
- internal/e2e/workflows/daemon_refresh_test.go
- internal/e2e/workflows/daemon_signals_test.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:46.649926900Z","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:08:08.341330218Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:08:08.341330218Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E tests for daemon lifecycle, signals, and refresh workflows.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-czda","depends_on_id":"caam-uc1a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:46.663935813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"caam-l19o.1.28","title":"Task: TUI preferences config + docs","description":"Goal
- Centralize all TUI-specific preferences (theme, motion, toasts, mouse, key hints, density) in a single config surface with clear precedence and docs.

Background
- The Glamorous TUI work introduces multiple toggles (NO_COLOR, reduced motion, toasts, mouse, non-TTY fallback). Without a unified config, defaults and overrides are inconsistent and hard to discover.

Details
- Add a `tui` section in the main config file with documented defaults.
- Provide CLI flags and env overrides for the most important toggles:
  - theme: auto|dark|light
  - high-contrast: bool
  - reduced-motion: bool
  - toasts: bool
  - mouse: bool
  - density: cozy|compact
  - show-key-hints: bool
  - no-tui: bool (force non-TTY mode)
- Define precedence order: CLI flags > env vars > config file > defaults.
- Add a `caam config tui` or `caam tui --help` section that prints the effective config.
- Update README and TUI help to document toggles and examples.
- Add structured debug logs showing resolved TUI config (redacting anything sensitive).

Success
- Users can reliably tune the TUI without hunting for hidden flags, and defaults remain sensible.

 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.3","title":"Task: Modal overlays + focus rings","description":"Goal
- Render dialogs as true overlays with a dimmed background and clear focus indication.

Background
- Current dialog overlay discards the underlying view; there is no context or focus ring, which feels non-premium and disorienting.
- Skill guidance includes modal overlay patterns using lipgloss.Place/Overlay and double borders.

Details
- Implement an overlay that composes the dialog on top of the main view (retain background context).
- Add a subtle dim or desaturation layer to the background to emphasize focus.
- Use a premium border style for the active modal (double/thick) and a focused input cursor style.
- Clamp modal width/height to the viewport; support small-screen fallback.
- Add a keyboard hint line inside the modal with styled keycaps.

Tests
- Golden snapshots for modal overlay in compact + full layouts.
- Unit tests for size clamping and overlay positioning.
- E2E TUI test that opens dialogs and verifies background retention + focus ring.
- Structured test logs with modal dimensions and layout mode.

Success
- Dialogs feel modal, focused, and visually distinct without losing context.
","notes":"Depends on: caam-l19o.1.1 (theme system) and caam-ftpx (existing focus-indicator UX task).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:30.554154643Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:04:10.764394626Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T10:04:10.764394626Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ftpx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:37.459041520Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:30.555736562Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-l19o.1.3","depends_on_id":"caam-l19o.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:11:34.189195020Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-l19o.1.30","title":"Task: List + detail visual polish (rows, badges, hierarchy)","description":"Goal
- Make list rows and detail panels feel structured, scannable, and premium.

Background
- Current list rows are functional but visually flat; detail sections are dense and not clearly grouped.
- Glamorous TUI guidance emphasizes hierarchy, spacing, badges, and consistent row anatomy.

Details
- Standardize list row anatomy: left icon, primary label, secondary metadata, right-side status chip.
- Add subtle zebra striping or row separators; ensure selection + focus are clearly distinct.
- Use status badges (plan/status) with consistent colors and padding.
- Improve detail panel with labeled sections (Profile, Auth, Usage, Paths) using consistent spacing and divider rules.
- Right-align numeric values and keep units consistent (e.g., minutes, days, GB).
- Apply truncation rules with ellipsis for long names; show full value on focus where possible.

Tests
- Add golden snapshots for list rows + detail sections and truncation behavior (see caam-l19o.1.32).
- Emit structured test logs with row width, truncation decisions, and badge styles.

Success
- Lists are easy to scan, and details read like a structured card rather than a wall of text.

+## TUI Configuration
+
+Customize the TUI appearance and behavior through `~/.caam/config.yaml`:
+
+```yaml
+tui:
+  theme: auto          # auto | dark | light
+  high_contrast: false # Enable high-contrast colors for accessibility
+  reduced_motion: false # Disable animated UI effects (spinners)
+  toasts: true         # Show transient notification messages
+  mouse: true          # Enable mouse support
+  show_key_hints: true # Show keyboard shortcuts in status bar
+  density: cozy        # cozy | compact
+  no_tui: false        # Disable TUI, use CLI-only mode
+```
+
+### Environment Variable Overrides
+
+Environment variables take precedence over config file settings:
+
+| Variable | Values | Description |
+|----------|--------|-------------|
+| `CAAM_TUI_THEME` | `auto`, `dark`, `light` | Color scheme |
+| `CAAM_TUI_CONTRAST` | `high`, `hc`, `1`, `true` | High contrast mode |
+| `CAAM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION` | `true`, `false` | Disable animations |
+| `REDUCED_MOTION` | `1` | Standard accessibility env var |
+| `CAAM_TUI_TOASTS` | `true`, `false` | Toast notifications |
+| `CAAM_TUI_MOUSE` | `true`, `false` | Mouse support |
+| `CAAM_TUI_KEY_HINTS` | `true`, `false` | Keyboard hints |
+| `CAAM_TUI_DENSITY` | `cozy`, `compact` | UI spacing |
+| `CAAM_NO_TUI` or `NO_TUI` | `true`, `1` | Disable TUI entirely |
+
+### Managing TUI Config via CLI
+
+```bash
+# View all TUI settings
+caam config tui
+
+# View a specific setting
+caam config tui theme
+caam config tui density
+
+# Change settings
+caam config tui theme dark
+caam config tui density compact
+caam config tui high_contrast true
+```
+
+---
+
 ## FAQ
 
 **Q: Does this work with API keys / pay-per-token plans?**

+func getTUIValue(t *config.TUIConfig, field string) (string, error) {
+	switch field {
+	case "theme":
+		return t.Theme, nil
+	case "high_contrast":
+		return strconv.FormatBool(t.HighContrast), nil
+	case "reduced_motion":
+		return strconv.FormatBool(t.ReducedMotion), nil
+	case "toasts":
+		return strconv.FormatBool(t.Toasts), nil
+	case "mouse":
+		return strconv.FormatBool(t.Mouse), nil
+	case "show_key_hints":
+		return strconv.FormatBool(t.ShowKeyHints), nil
+	case "density":
+		return t.Density, nil
+	case "no_tui":
+		return strconv.FormatBool(t.NoTUI), nil
+	default:
+		return "", fmt.Errorf("unknown tui field: %s", field)
+	}
+}
+
+func setTUIValue(t *config.TUIConfig, field, value string) error {
+	switch field {
+	case "theme":
+		theme := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(value))
+		switch theme {
+		case "auto", "dark", "light":
+			t.Theme = theme
+		default:
+			return fmt.Errorf("invalid theme: %s (use auto, dark, or light)", value)
+		}
+	case "high_contrast":
+		b, err := parseBool(value)
+		if err != nil {
+			return err
+		}
+		t.HighContrast = b
+	case "reduced_motion":
+		b, err := parseBool(value)
+		if err != nil {
+			return err
+		}
+		t.ReducedMotion = b
+	case "toasts":
+		b, err := parseBool(value)
+		if err != nil {
+			return err
+		}
+		t.Toasts = b
+	case "mouse":
+		b, err := parseBool(value)
+		if err != nil {
+			return err
+		}
+		t.Mouse = b
+	case "show_key_hints":
+		b, err := parseBool(value)
+		if err != nil {
+			return err
+		}
+		t.ShowKeyHints = b
+	case "density":
+		density := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(value))
+		switch density {
+		case "cozy", "compact":
+			t.Density = density
+		default:
+			return fmt.Errorf("invalid density: %s (use cozy or compact)", value)
+		}
+	case "no_tui":
+		b, err := parseBool(value)
+		if err != nil {
+			return err
+		}
+		t.NoTUI = b
+	default:
+		return fmt.Errorf("unknown tui field: %s", field)
+	}
+	return nil
+}
+
+// configTUICmd shows and manages TUI preferences.
+var configTUICmd = &cobra.Command{
+	Use:   "tui [key] [value]",
+	Short: "View and modify TUI preferences",
+	Long: `View and modify TUI appearance and behavior preferences.
+
+When called without arguments, shows all TUI settings.
+When called with a key, shows that specific setting.
+When called with a key and value, sets that setting.
+
+Available keys:
+  theme          Color scheme: auto (default), dark, light
+  high_contrast  Enable high-contrast colors (bool)
+  reduced_motion Disable animations like spinners (bool)
+  toasts         Show transient notifications (bool)
+  mouse          Enable mouse support (bool)
+  show_key_hints Show keyboard shortcuts in status bar (bool)
+  density        Spacing: cozy (default), compact
+  no_tui         Disable TUI entirely (bool)
+
+Environment variable overrides (higher priority than config):
+  CAAM_TUI_THEME          Theme setting
+  CAAM_TUI_CONTRAST       high or normal
+  CAAM_TUI_REDUCED_MOTION Disable animations
+  CAAM_TUI_TOASTS         Show toasts
+  CAAM_TUI_MOUSE          Mouse support
+  CAAM_TUI_KEY_HINTS      Key hints
+  CAAM_TUI_DENSITY        cozy or compact
+  CAAM_NO_TUI / NO_TUI    Disable TUI
+
+Examples:
+  caam config tui                          # Show all TUI settings
+  caam config tui theme                    # Show theme setting
+  caam config tui theme dark               # Set theme to dark
+  caam config tui reduced_motion true      # Disable animations
+  caam config tui density compact          # Use compact spacing`,
+	Args: cobra.MaximumNArgs(2),
+	RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+		if len(args) == 0 {
+			// Show all TUI settings
+			fmt.Println("TUI Preferences")
+			fmt.Println("───────────────────────────────────────────")
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %s\n", "theme:", spmConfig.TUI.Theme)
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %t\n", "high_contrast:", spmConfig.TUI.HighContrast)
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %t\n", "reduced_motion:", spmConfig.TUI.ReducedMotion)
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %t\n", "toasts:", spmConfig.TUI.Toasts)
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %t\n", "mouse:", spmConfig.TUI.Mouse)
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %t\n", "show_key_hints:", spmConfig.TUI.ShowKeyHints)
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %s\n", "density:", spmConfig.TUI.Density)
+			fmt.Printf("  %-16s %t\n", "no_tui:", spmConfig.TUI.NoTUI)
+			fmt.Println()
+			fmt.Printf("Config file: %s\n", config.SPMConfigPath())
+			return nil
+		}
+
+		key := "tui." + args[0]
+		if len(args) == 1 {
+			// Get specific value
+			value, err := getConfigValue(spmConfig, key)
+			if err != nil {
+				return err
+			}
+			fmt.Println(value)
+			return nil
+		}
+
+		// Set value
+		value := args[1]
+		if err := setConfigValue(spmConfig, key, value); err != nil {
+			return err
+		}
+
+		if err := spmConfig.Save(); err != nil {
+			return fmt.Errorf("save config: %w", err)
+		}
+
+		// Show updated value
+		newValue, _ := getConfigValue(spmConfig, key)
+		fmt.Printf("tui.%s = %s\n", args[0], newValue)
+		return nil
+	},
+}
+
